title: rubus rosaceus subsp. histrix
intro: Rubus rosaceus subsp. histrix, also known as the thorny raspberry, is a subspecies of the Rubus rosaceus plant, which is a type of thorny shrub that produces edible fruits. The medicinal properties of Rubus rosaceus subsp. histrix include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The plant has been used to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support cardiovascular health, among other health benefits. In herbal preparations, Rubus rosaceus subsp. histrix is often used to make teas, tinctures, and jams, and its fruits can be consumed fresh or dried to reap its nutritional benefits.
